posts.ig_caption:
🌿 Motivo Te Pā de hoje: Atualoa (Centipede) | Samoa / Oceania

Força, resistência e compromisso com a família extensa (aiga). A centopeia é venenosa e simboliza o perigo e o poder; representa também a devoção familiar. Seu renascimento no siapo faz parte dos movimentos de recuperação cultural.

✊ Resistência da comunidade indígena à extração de dados

Feso'ota'iga fa'alapotopotoga, taumafaiga fa'atasi, ma le fagofago. O le upega e fa'atusalia ai upega fa'aleaganu'u. I le tatau, e fa'aalia ai le feso'ota'iga o le nu'u ma le fesoasoani fa'atasi.

✊ Tetee o nu'u aganuu i le tango o fa'amaumauga

pt.meaning: Identidade de clã, patente e proteção espiritual. Cada sub-clã (tokatoka) possui motivos de masi específicos (draudrau) que são sua propriedade intelectual. A técnica de estêncil é única em Fiji. Os motivos codificam genealogia de clã e reivindicações territoriais (vanua).

posts.ig_caption:
🌿 Today's Te Pā motif: Yvy Marã Eÿ (Land Without Evil) | Guaraní / Paraguay

The mythical Guaraní paradise — a land free of hunger, war, and disease. The soul must achieve a state of perfection (aguyjé) to reach it. Associated with eastward migration, prophetic leadership, and shamanic song. Widely used in APIB rhetoric and land-rights manifestos as the original indigenous claim to territorial sovereignty.

✊ Indigenous community resistance to data extraction

posts.ig_caption:
🌿 Ko te hōpua o tēnei rā: Yvy Marã Eÿ (Land Without Evil) | Guaraní

The mythical Guaraní paradise — a land free of hunger, war, and disease. The soul must achieve a state of perfection (aguyjé) to reach it. Associated with eastward migration, prophetic leadership, and shamanic song. Widely used in APIB rhetoric and land-rights manifestos as the original indigenous claim to territorial sovereignty.

✊ Te tūkino hapori Māori ki te tāhekenga raraunga

📚 Mō te akomanga: Kōrerotia: me pēhea tēnei hōpua e ako ana i ngā mātāpono mana motuhake raraunga?

posts.ig_caption:
🌿 Ko te hōpua o tēnei rā: Cuaracy Ra'Angaba (Solar Calendar / Image of the Sun) | Guaraní

An astronomical stone calendar used by the Guaraní to track seasons, solstices, moon phases, and cardinal directions. 'Cuaracy' means 'sun' and 'Ra'angaba' means 'image.' The sun is the greatest deity (Ñamandu); the clock encodes the sacred eternal circle and continuity of life.

✊ Te tūkino hapori Māori ki te tāhekenga raraunga

posts.ig_caption:
🌿 Motivo Te Pā de hoje: Ronin Kuin (Primordial Anaconda / Cosmic Serpent) | Shipibo-Conibo / Peru

A anaconda primordial que cantou o universo à existência na cosmologia shipibo-conibo. O padrão de sua pele é o modelo para todos os kené. Ela é a 'mãe dos desenhos' (rono ewa). O motivo anaconda é usado em campanhas ambientais para simbolizar a teia ecológica destruída pelo garimpo.

✊ Resistência da comunidade indígena à extração de dados

📚 Sala de aula: Discuta: como este motivo ensina princípios de soberania de dados?

🔗 Kit de ensino: https://pub-bf8eea881c1e44d88eda5192c3b92291.r2.dev/teaching-kits/te-pa-teaching-kit-pt.pdf
🌐 Plataforma: https://kiwi-dialectic-te-pa-minisite.vercel.app/rhizome-mapper.html

posts.ig_caption:
🌿 Today's Te Pā motif: Mola (Guna Reverse Appliqué Panel) | Guna / Panama

Hand-stitched textile panels worn by Guna women of the San Blas Archipelago (Guna Yala), Panama. Its designs may be residuals of pre-Hispanic body-painting patterns. Traditional abstract designs are based on brain coral — the sea as cosmic reference for an archipelago people. The Guna achieved autonomy (Guna Yala comarca) partly through cultural mobilisation in which mola was central.

✊ Indigenous community resistance to data extraction

📚 Classroom: Discuss: how does this motif teach data sovereignty principles?

sm.meaning: Ni'o textile a tamaitai Guna o Arquipélago San Blas, Panamá. O fuafuaga o le fa'ailoga e ono'ai mai tulaga o le tino painting a tua. O fuafuaga geometric abstract e faavae i luga o fatu sami. Na mafai ai ona maua le pule o Guna Yala i se vaega ona o ola fa'aaganu'u e aofia ai le mola.

posts.ig_caption:
🌿 Today's Te Pā motif: Kayapó Jaguar Body Division Pattern | Kayapó / Brazil

The jaguar is the apex predator and spiritual power animal of Kayapó cosmology. Jaguar-pattern body paint marks warriors, chiefs, and ceremony participants. Bilateral body division into upper (red, vital, social) and lower (black, wild, nature) halves encodes the Kayapó cosmological opposition between society and the forest.

✊ Indigenous community resistance to data extraction

pt.meaning: A onça é o animal-espírito por excelência dos Kayapó. A divisão bilateral do corpo em metade vermelha (superior — social, vital) e metade preta (inferior — natureza selvagem) codifica a cosmologia Kayapó. Líderes como Raoni Metuktire aparecem na mídia com esta pintura em cúpulas climáticas.

posts.ig_caption:
🌿 Today's Te Pā motif: Urihi a (Forest-Land Cosmos / Falling Sky) | Yanomami / Brazil

Urihi a ('forest-land') is the Yanomami concept for the inhabited world — the second lowest of four cosmic layers. Davi Kopenawa's cosmology, recorded in 'The Falling Sky,' describes the entire cosmos collapsing if the forest is destroyed. The phrase 'A queda do céu' became the central Yanomami political-cosmological text and is used in deforestation campaigns and UN submissions.

✊ Indigenous community resistance to data extraction

📚 Classroom: Discuss: how does this motif teach data sovereignty principles?

🌿 Today's Te Pā motif: Luta pela Vida (Struggle for Life — Political Body Paint) | Amazonian Peoples / Brazil

The Luta pela Vida (Struggle for Life) camp by APIB in Brasília (August–September 2021) was the largest indigenous mobilisation in Brazilian history. Body paint was simultaneously an act of cultural affirmation, political challenge, and territorial claim. #ForaGarimpoPoraCovid and #APIB circulated these images globally.

✊ Indigenous community resistance to data extraction
